task: I’m trying to decide whether to buy a used truck I found, but I don’t want to jump in and regret it later. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist for what I should verify before I spend the money?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **go / no-go checklist** you can use before buying a used truck.

## Go / No-Go Checklist

### 1) Title and paperwork
- **Go:** Clean title in seller’s name, VIN matches title/door jamb/dash
- **No-go:** Salvage, flood, rebuilt title unless you fully understand the risk
- **Go:** No liens, or lien is being properly cleared at sale
- **No-go:** Seller can’t prove ownership or has missing paperwork

### 2) Accident / damage history
- **Go:** No major frame damage, flood damage, or airbag deployment
- **No-go:** Signs of serious collision repair, rust repair, or water damage
- **Check:** Uneven panel gaps, mismatched paint, overspray, bent frame, rust in odd places

### 3) Engine and transmission
- **Go:** Starts easily, idles smoothly, no warning lights
- **No-go:** Hard starting, knocking, slipping transmission, smoke, overheating
- **Check:** Fluid condition and leaks under the truck

### 4) 4x4 / drivetrain
- **Go:** If 4WD, it engages and disengages properly
- **No-go:** Grinding, binding, or dashboard warnings when using 4WD
- **Check:** Driveshafts, transfer case, differential leaks/noise

### 5) Suspension and steering
- **Go:** Drives straight, no major clunks, no severe tire wear
- **No-go:** Excessive wandering, vibration, sagging, or steering play
- **Check:** Ball joints, tie rods, shocks, wheel bearings

### 6) Brakes and tires
- **Go:** Good tread, even wear, brakes feel firm
- **No-go:** Bald tires, pulsing brake pedal, grinding, pulling to one side
- **Check:** Tire age too — old tires can be unsafe even with tread

### 7) Rust and underside condition
- **Go:** Light surface rust only
- **No-go:** Frame rust, holes, flaky structural rust, brake/fuel line corrosion
- **Check:** Cab mounts, frame rails, rocker panels, bed supports

### 8) Electrical and accessories
- **Go:** Lights, windows, locks, HVAC, gauges, backup camera all work
- **No-go:** Multiple electrical issues, dead modules, flickering lights
- **Check:** Battery, alternator, dashboard warning lights

### 9) Test drive
- **Go:** Cold start, highway speeds, braking, turning, backing up all feel normal
- **No-go:** Any overheating, strange noises, vibrations, or warning messages
- **Check:** Test drive long enough to get it fully warm

### 10) Maintenance records
- **Go:** Regular oil changes and service history
- **No-go:** No records at all, or signs of neglect
- **Check:** Timing belt/chain service, transmission service, coolant, brakes

### 11) Price vs. condition
- **Go:** Price makes sense after considering repairs and market value
- **No-go:** Cheap truck with expensive hidden problems
- **Rule:** If repairs could exceed your buffer, walk away

### 12) Independent inspection
- **Go:** A trusted mechanic inspects it and finds no major issues
- **No-go:** Seller refuses inspection or rushes the sale

## Simple decision rule
- **Buy it** only if:
  - title is clean,
  - no major rust/accident damage,
  - it passes a test drive,
  - and a mechanic says it’s sound.

- **Walk away** if:
  - there’s frame rust,
  - title problems,
  - transmission issues,
  - flood/salvage history,
  - or the seller won’t let you inspect it.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** or a **yes/no scorecard** you can use while looking at the truck.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s solid. Clean and usable. Covers the stuff that actually matters — title, rust, drivetrain, records, inspection. No fluff.

I’d use it as-is, maybe add one line for **cooling system** since that’s bitten me before. On a used truck, if it overheats on a test drive or the coolant looks neglected, that’s a hard no for me.

Otherwise, good checklist.
